short_summary: Connects artificial to Latin artificialis/artificium and to art, skill, theory/system, and the maker root.
notes: Use as semantic-family evidence, not as final earliest-quotation proof.

short_summary: Reports artificial day as an early English phrase for the daylight span, contrasted with the full natural day.
notes: Useful technical/reckoning anchor but likely too obscure without extra context.

short_summary: Gives artifice first as workmanship and making by craft or skill, with trick/device later.
Gives artificer as one who makes by art or skill, strengthening the maker/craftsperson branch.
Places made or contrived by art/human skill before fictitious/not-genuine senses, and gives technical examples.
notes: Best public historical dictionary anchor collected in this pass.

short_summary: Also records feigned, fictitious, and not genuine/natural as a separate sense.
notes: Useful for coexistence rather than a simple replacement story.

short_summary: Frames art as human skill, rule-governed action, mechanic/liberal arts, and learned dexterity.
notes: Key support for explaining art in artificial as skill/technique.

short_summary: Defines artifice as an ingenious device in good or bad sense, and as art/trade/skill by practice.
notes: Good for showing contrivance is not automatically deception.

short_summary: Defines artificer as skilled artist/mechanic/manufacturer and maker/contriver.
notes: Strong maker noun; may be too specialized for main visual text.

short_summary: Rhetorical artificial arguments are invented by the speaker, contrasted with authority/testimony-type proofs.
notes: Shows artificial as made by rhetorical art, not fake.

source_name: Rhetorica ad Herennium passages on memory
short_summary: Distinguishes natural memory from artificial memory as training/system/discipline that strengthens memory.
notes: Conceptually strong but not an English lexical attestation.

short_summary: Summarizes classical/medieval rhetoric as distinguishing innate natural memory from rule-cultivated artificial memory.
notes: Good scholarly corroboration; full chapter may be paywalled.

short_summary: Modern entry keeps human-made/natural-model and not-genuine/sincere branches, with etymology from skill/craft/making.
notes: Good for modern sense split and etymology, not early chronology.

short_summary: Modern entry separates made by people/often copy of nature from not sincere.
notes: Useful contrast source; not an early-history source.

short_summary: Modern learner entry separates artificial, synthetic, false, man-made, fake, and imitation in the synonym field.
notes: Useful for later precision: not natural does not automatically mean fake.
